outlook.summary: Not financial advice — this is a read of the public data and news above, meant to help you decide, not a recommendation. Always size positions and confirm with your own research.

strongest.reason: The pullback looks like profit-taking after an 80%+ H1 rally, not a fundamentals problem — Nvidia now trades at ~15x FY2028 earnings while still growing 85% YoY. Historically the highest-conviction theme in this watchlist.
Showing resilience despite geopolitical noise, with ETF inflows just turning positive again after a rough June. Macro-sensitive, but the trend this week is constructive.

weakest.reason: The 'agentic AI eats SaaS' narrative is driving a structural repricing — good earnings (ServiceNow) are still getting sold. This looks like it needs a clear counter-narrative before it stabilizes, not just one strong quarter.

Real commercialization (Optimus external sales, Unitree profit) is lagging the stock price moves, which are largely headline-driven. Higher volatility, lower conviction until production numbers catch up to the hype.

outlook.nextStep: Given the setup, the chips/AI-infra pullback is the area most worth a closer look if you're considering adding exposure — it's sentiment-driven rather than fundamentals-driven. AI software and robotics are better watched than acted on right now until their respective narratives (AI disruption fears, actual commercial deployment) resolve one way or the other.

signals.title: Chips pulling back after an 80%+ H1 run
signals.body: Broadcom's Q3 AI guidance ($16B) missed the $17.2B estimate and didn't raise full-year forecasts, a sell-the-news drop of ~14% on June 4. Micron fell ~10% on similar jitters despite being up 260%+ YTD. Nvidia is cheaper now at ~15x FY2028 earnings, still growing revenue 85% YoY.
signals.watch: Hyperscaler capex commentary (MSFT/GOOGL/AMZN earnings) and any signs the data-center buildout is slowing.
signals.focus: Worth a closer look — this reads as profit-taking, not a fundamentals break.

signals.title: Fed and oil are the near-term wildcards
signals.body: New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh held rates steady in June; a hike is still on the table this year. Renewed U.S.-Iran strikes have pushed oil (WTI ~$71.41) and the 10-year yield (~4.57%, highest since May) up, adding pressure on growth/tech valuations.
signals.watch: This week's CPI print and any Strait of Hormuz escalation.
signals.focus: This is the swing factor for everything else — a hawkish surprise or oil spike would hit growth names across every tier.

signals.title: AI software selloff looks structural, not a blip
signals.body: Enterprise software has been sold off since January on fears agentic AI shrinks software seat counts. ServiceNow beat estimates (+22% revenue YoY) and still dropped 18% on the print.
signals.watch: Whether any name can decouple from the AI-eats-SaaS narrative with a strong quarter.
signals.focus: Lower conviction right now — better to watch for a narrative shift than act on today's prices.

signals.title: Robotics is hype-driven and headline-sensitive
signals.body: Humanoid names spiked July 10 on Tesla Optimus procurement orders and Unitree's IPO approval. Tesla's 2026 Optimus units are for internal testing only; Unitree's profit fell 52% YoY even as revenue grew.
signals.watch: This sector can gap hard on single headlines in either direction.
signals.focus: Speculative — size any position smaller given the gap between stock moves and actual commercial traction.

signals.title: Crypto resilient but macro-tied
signals.body: BTC/ETH are up ~2.8% over the past week despite the Iran conflict. Spot BTC ETFs just snapped a 10-day outflow streak with a $221.7M inflow day, the largest in two months.
signals.watch: Further Strait of Hormuz developments and ETF flow trends.
signals.focus: Constructive trend this week, but stay mindful of geopolitical headline risk.

signals.title: CRWD trading near its stop-loss
signals.body: Current price $187.18 versus the tracked stop at $184, down 5.66% today. Still above the stop, but support is being tested.
signals.watch: Whether 184 holds, or whether the broader software selloff narrative starts pulling security names down too.
signals.focus: Worth checking daily until it clears back above $190 or confirms the stop.
